Writing a Simple R Package Using S3 Methods

Building a Table 1 function with S3 dispatch to handle different variable types automatically

r
package-development
object-oriented

I did not really know how S3 method dispatch worked in R until I tried to build a Table 1 function that handled numeric and categorical variables differently without writing separate code paths.

Author

Ronald ‘Ryy’ G. Thomas

Published

July 10, 2025

S3 dispatch lets one function name do the right thing for each variable type.

1 Introduction

[YOUR CONTENT HERE – 2-3 paragraphs. Open with an “I didn’t really know…” hook about S3 methods. Describe the problem that led you to learn S3 (e.g., wanting a single function that summarises both numeric and categorical variables for a Table 1). Transition to what this post will accomplish.]

1.1 Motivations

[YOUR CONTENT HERE – 4-6 bullet points. Personal and specific reasons for learning S3. Examples: frustration with if/else chains for variable types, wanting cleaner package code, reading Nick Tierney’s paper, etc.]

1.2 Objectives

  1. [YOUR CONTENT HERE – specific, measurable]
  2. [YOUR CONTENT HERE]
  3. [YOUR CONTENT HERE]
  4. [YOUR CONTENT HERE]

I am documenting my learning process here. If you spot errors or have better approaches, please let me know.

From scattered functions to a structured package.

2 Prerequisites and Setup

[YOUR CONTENT HERE – 1-2 paragraphs listing what readers need: R 4.0+, devtools, roxygen2, testthat, palmerpenguins. Show library loading code.]

library(tidyverse)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(knitr)

set.seed(101)
dat <- palmerpenguins::penguins |>
  filter(!is.na(sex))

3 What is S3 Object-Oriented Programming?

[YOUR CONTENT HERE – 1-2 paragraphs in plain language. Define S3 dispatch: when you call a generic function like print(), R looks at the class of the object and calls the appropriate method. Use an analogy. Explain UseMethod(). Transition to the Table 1 use case.]

4 Getting Started: A Simple Generic Function

[YOUR INTERPRETATION HERE]

Understanding S3 dispatch is the foundation for writing extensible R packages.

4.1 Building the Table 1 Function

[YOUR CONTENT HERE – The core analysis section. Show how to build a table1() generic that dispatches to methods for numeric variables (mean, SD, range) and categorical variables (counts, percentages). Use the Palmer Penguins dataset as the example.]

8 See Also

Related posts:

  • [YOUR RELATED POST 1]
  • [YOUR RELATED POST 2]

Key resources:

  • A Simple Guide to S3 Methods (Tierney) – The ArXiv paper that inspired this post
  • Advanced R: S3 (Wickham) – Comprehensive S3 reference
  • R Packages (Wickham & Bryan) – The definitive guide to R package development
  • devtools documentation – Tools for R package development
  • Palmer Penguins Dataset – The dataset used in the examples

10 Let’s Connect

I would enjoy hearing from you if:

  • You spot an error or a better approach to any of the code in this post.
  • You have suggestions for topics you would like to see covered.
  • You want to discuss R programming, data science, or reproducible research.
  • You have questions about anything in this tutorial.
  • You just want to say hello and connect.
